concepts_list.name: domain_Omega
concepts_list.description: The main computational domain in the physical space
concepts_list.justification: The domain of interest is defined as a region Ω ⊂ R^d, and it is described as the region of interest in the problem setup.

concepts_list.name: boundary_Gamma
concepts_list.description: The boundary of the domain Ω, composed of Dirichlet and Neumann parts
concepts_list.justification: The domain boundary ∂Ω ≡ Γ is introduced and decomposed into Γ_u and Γ_t, representing different types of boundary conditions.

concepts_list.name: d_Omega
concepts_list.description: Differential element of volume integration over the domain Ω
concepts_list.justification: Appears in integrals over Ω as 'dΩ' in both the thermal and elastostatics weak forms.

concepts_list.name: d_Gamma
concepts_list.description: Differential element of surface integration over the boundary Γ
concepts_list.justification: Appears in integrals over Γ_t as 'dΓ' in the boundary conditions for heat flux and traction.

concepts_list.name: displacement_field_u
concepts_list.description: Primal unknown field, representing temperature or displacement depending on the problem context
concepts_list.justification: The variable u is used to denote the temperature field in thermal problems and displacement in elastostatics, and is the unknown to be found in both cases.

concepts_list.name: thermal_conductivity_kappa
concepts_list.justification: Defined as κ, used in the thermal conductivity term ∇κ∇u in the weak form.

concepts_list.name: heat_source_f
concepts_list.description: Volume heat source term in the thermal problem
concepts_list.justification: The heat source f appears in the right-hand side of the weak form as ∫_Ω v f dΩ.

concepts_list.name: heat_flux_q_bar
concepts_list.description: Prescribed heat flux on the Neumann boundary Γ_t
concepts_list.justification: The heat flux q̄ is prescribed on Γ_t and appears in the boundary integral term ∫_{Γ_t} v q̄ dΓ.

concepts_list.name: function_space_V
concepts_list.description: Test function space for the weak formulation
concepts_list.justification: V is the space of test functions used in the weak form, with v ∈ V as the test function in both equations (1) and (2).

concepts_list.name: neumann_boundary_Gamma_t
concepts_list.description: Part of the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions are prescribed
concepts_list.justification: Γ_t is explicitly defined as the boundary where Neumann conditions (e.g., heat flux or traction) are prescribed.

new_concept_info.description:
The main computational domain in the physical space
JUSTIFICATION:
The domain of interest is defined as a region Ω ⊂ R^d, and it is described as the region of interest in the problem setup.

Differential element of volume integration over the domain Ω
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears in integrals over Ω as 'dΩ' in both the thermal and elastostatics weak forms.

Differential element of surface integration over the boundary Γ
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears in integrals over Γ_t as 'dΓ' in the boundary conditions for heat flux and traction.

Primal unknown field, representing temperature or displacement depending on the problem context
JUSTIFICATION:
The variable u is used to denote the temperature field in thermal problems and displacement in elastostatics, and is the unknown to be found in both cases.

Material property representing thermal conductivity
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as κ, used in the thermal conductivity term ∇κ∇u in the weak form.

Volume heat source term in the thermal problem
JUSTIFICATION:
The heat source f appears in the right-hand side of the weak form as ∫_Ω v f dΩ.

Prescribed heat flux on the Neumann boundary Γ_t
JUSTIFICATION:
The heat flux q̄ is prescribed on Γ_t and appears in the boundary integral term ∫_{Γ_t} v q̄ dΓ.

Test function space for the weak formulation
JUSTIFICATION:
V is the space of test functions used in the weak form, with v ∈ V as the test function in both equations (1) and (2).

Part of the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions are prescribed
JUSTIFICATION:
Γ_t is explicitly defined as the boundary where Neumann conditions (e.g., heat flux or traction) are prescribed.
